checklist_summary.note: 35 raw items from 2 generator batches; Phase 1.5 deduper found no cross-batch duplicates.

checklist.claim: analyzer launches no verification command / no subprocess (AC #527-2)
checklist.evidence: no subprocess/os.system/os.exec*/os.spawn*/fork/multiprocessing/eval/ctypes call sites; traced every wfr.* call site - parse_events/detect_occurrences/load_registry never reach _observe_git/_run_git

checklist.claim: provisional/unknown rows never promoted, never silently omitted
checklist.evidence: row-presence half TRUE; but extract_verification_lifecycles filters only on source==LOCAL and source_status==AVAILABLE with NO eligibility check, so an ineligible-but-importable row contributes launches to the numerator while its eligibility bucket records confirmed_ineligible - live repro: eligibility_state_bounds{confirmed_ineligible:1,confirmed_eligible:0} with local_actual_launches=1

checklist.claim: only explicit lifecycle+source-event identities produce exact join confidence
checklist.evidence: empirically: empty-string lifecycle_ids short-circuit the falsy guard and fall to partial, never exact

checklist.claim: distinct lifecycle IDs / run attempts / bindings / consumer roles / iterations / checkpoints / commits / merges / retriggers cannot be transport-retry candidates
checklist.evidence: _classify_relationship never reads consumer_skill; empirically two members sharing lifecycle_id+digest+workspace with DIFFERENT consumer_skill returned ('candidate_transport_retry','exact') - the AC's consumer-roles dimension was foreclosed by nothing

checklist.claim: absent/incomplete cloud census makes coverage unavailable, never zero
checklist.evidence: build_cloud_census(None,{}) -> unavailable:True; pagination_complete=False -> unavailable:True; genuinely-empty complete window -> available:True (distinct)

checklist.claim: secret redaction: no raw secret and no unkeyed digest of secret material persists, for every recognized pattern class
checklist.evidence: backslash-escaped whitespace is not a word boundary in shell but the bare-char class stops at it: API_KEY=my\ secret\ value -> 'API_KEY=<env:API_KEY> secret\ value' leaks into redacted_display AND the digest with secret_affected=True; also --flag, -u halves, and escaped quotes inside dquotes

checklist.claim: redaction regexes are ReDoS-safe (quote-exclusion prevents exponential backtracking)
checklist.evidence: max 1.1ms across quote-dense/colon-less adversarial inputs up to 20k chars

phase3_findings.description: Malformed run rows defeat the exporter's drop guard: build_snapshot's isinstance guard is unreachable from the real fetch path. With --workflows set the comprehension discards the non-dict uncounted (measured pagination_complete=True dropped_run_count=0 - a shape-drifted census self-certifying complete over a shrunk denominator); with --workflows empty the non-dict reaches run.get('id') -> AttributeError.
documented_falsehood: the diff-added claim 'a shape-drifted census never self-certifies complete' is false against HEAD given the above. Self-contradicting diff -> non-demotable REJECT carve-out.
eligible_lifecycles counts confirmed-ineligible rows (5x inflation measured: reports 5, actual 1). Site 2743 (stdout summary) prints the inflated figure with no adjacent bounds disclosure.
consumer_approximate is dropped by its only reader (load_cloud_mappings builds entries from three keys), so a stratifier cannot tell devflow.yml's multiplexed command attribution from an exact one.

matcher-probe.yml's five claude-code-action jobs are absent from cloud_mappings.agent_jobs with no documented exclusion rationale, unlike the documented carve-outs for devflow-runner.yml and devflow.yml. Independent signal: grep -l claude-code-action over .github/workflows/*.yml.

The run.sh subprocess-absence pins' regex alternations are blind to os.exec*/os.spawn*/os.fork/multiprocessing/asyncio.create_subprocess_*/eval/ctypes. Latent forward-looking guard gap - the module contains none of these today.

The two new run.sh absence pins are plain assert_eq grep-count checks rather than routed through assert_pin_red_under, bypassing the repo's pin-auditability convention. Verified mutation-provably correct by hand.

PR description claims '37 unittests' and '5 mutation tests'; actual is 133 test_ methods and 6 mutation tests. PR body only - no tracked diff-added file repeats the counts, so not a self-contradicting-diff carve-out subject.

EligibleLifecycle validates the source/source_status pairing once at construction but source_status is mutated in place afterward with no re-validation; latent, both mutation sites assign in-set values.

frozen=True is one level shallow: dict/list fields (timing, workspace_state, provenance, members) remain mutable in place, so the design's 'nothing mutates them after construction' claim is not literally true for nested containers. No live call site mutates them.
RelationshipGroup.__post_init__ does not assert len(members) >= 1; an empty-members group would silently produce duration_ms=None rather than raising. Latent - group_launches always passes non-empty.

fix_decisions.claim_text: matcher-probe.yml's five claude-code-action jobs are absent from agent_jobs with no documented rationale, contradicting the registry's 'ineligible by omission' framing
fix_decisions.evidence: Over-grade gate shape 2 (diagnostic-only, no behavioral fail-direction): the registry's sentence 'any job whose (workflow_file, job) is not listed below is ineligible by omission' is LITERALLY TRUE of matcher-probe's jobs - they are ineligible, by omission, exactly as stated - so the finding is not a documented_falsehood and the kind is mis-filed. Independently verified: matcher-probe.yml is repo-internal probe infrastructure, is not shipped by install.sh, and runs no consumer lifecycle, so its omission is correct; the gap is a missing rationale, i.e. documentation completeness with no wrong output, no corrupted state, no skipped guard. Recorded technical evaluation, did not auto-demote. Fixed anyway (cheap, and it makes oversight distinguishable from intent).

Real but below the important fix threshold, and latent by the critic's own measurement: the module contains none of these spellings today (verified by an independent read of its import list and call surface), so nothing is currently undetected. Widening the alternation is a forward-looking guard improvement, not a live defect.

fix_decisions.claim_text: the two new absence pins are plain assert_eq grep counts rather than assert_pin_red_under
fix_decisions.evidence: Below threshold and self-described by the reporting agent as a style/convention nit, not a functional gap: it independently mutation-verified both pins DO go RED under the guarded regression (a reintroduced subprocess call). The pins are non-vacuous; only their routing differs from convention.

fix_decisions.claim_text: PR description claims 37 unittests / 5 mutation tests; actual 133 / 6
fix_decisions.evidence: Below threshold. Verified the counts appear in NO tracked diff-added file (grep over the diff), so this is not a self-contradicting-diff carve-out subject - the PR body is not part of the reviewed diff. Corrected out-of-band in the PR description rather than by a code change.

fix_decisions.claim_text: frozen=True is shallow; nested dict/list fields remain mutable; EligibleLifecycle re-validation gap; RelationshipGroup non-empty members
fix_decisions.evidence: Three Low/type_design findings, all explicitly latent by the reporting agent's own grep: no live call site performs the in-place mutation any of them describes. Below the important threshold; a design hardening for a future extension, not a defect in this PR as shipped.

stale_prose_check.note: Exit 0 = no STALE rows, so nothing gates. UNRESOLVABLE rows are informational by engine contract. The repo extension's count-locked pin-or-don't-write policy was considered: every count-locked row here is an in-code comment describing local structure (a recognition-only bound), not a repo-wide enumeration whose drift a pin would catch; none were newly authored by this iteration's fix.

verification_evidence.note: 8260 passed, 0 failed. The summary carried NO skip line, which by the runner's own contract means zero self-skipped checks - a genuinely clean run, not a laundered one. Run on a CLEAN tree (committed first) so the #434 stale-prose self-scan actually executed rather than self-skipping on a dirty tree. Also: python3 lib/test/test_verification_baseline.py -> 143 tests exit 0; test_workflow_flight_recorder.py -> 48 tests exit 0; ruff clean; shellcheck clean.

test_first_gate.decision: automated test applies (redaction is a pure function with an observable return)
test_first_gate.evidence: Written before the fix. Failed for the right reason on all 5 subTests, each naming a real leaked fragment, e.g. "'secret' unexpectedly found in 'API_KEY= secret\\ value pytest'". Mutation MUT2 (neutralise _ESC_CHUNK) -> RED.

test_first_gate.decision: automated test applies (fetch layer is drivable with a stubbed _gh_json)
test_first_gate.evidence: Written before the fix. Failed with '0 != 1 : malformed run was discarded uncounted before build_snapshot' (the fail-open) and the sibling test with AttributeError at line 224 (the crash). Deliberately drives the real fetch path - the pre-existing exporter test called build_snapshot directly, bypassing the layer that swallowed the row. Mutation MUT3 -> RED.
Written before the fix. Failed 'None is not true : consumer_approximate dropped by load_cloud_mappings'. Mutation MUT4 -> RED.

Written before the fix. Failed 'candidate_transport_retry == candidate_transport_retry'. Carries TWO positive controls (same-consumer and unrecorded-None-consumer pairs must STILL reach candidate) so a guard that forecloses everything cannot pass. Mutations: MUT6 (remove the foreclosure) -> RED; MUT7 (over-broad guard admitting None, len>=1) -> controls RED.

test_first_gate.decision: GATE VIOLATION, disclosed: the I1/VC-2 metric fix was authored BEFORE its test
test_first_gate.result: GREEN on first run (test written after the fix)
test_first_gate.evidence: Recorded honestly rather than presented as a clean gate. Remediated with the stronger evidence the gate exists to obtain: mutation MUT1 (revert eligible_denominator to len(rows)) -> RED, and MUT5 (revert the schema bump) -> RED, both on fresh file copies with an `assert old in s` proving the mutation actually applied. So the pin is proven non-vacuous against the guarded regression even though the red-before-green ordering was not observed.

test_first_gate.decision: no automated test applies (registry comment prose - the matcher-probe carve-out record)
test_first_gate.command: adversarial dry-trace, scoped to the fix delta
test_first_gate.evidence: Phase-2.4-style trace: the added sentence makes three checkable claims, each verified against HEAD rather than asserted - (1) matcher-probe.yml runs claude-code-action [grep -l over .github/workflows/*.yml: confirmed]; (2) its jobs are probe/implement-probe/schedulewakeup-probe/execfile-shape-probe/hook-probe [job-key grep: confirmed]; (3) it is not shipped by install.sh [grep install.sh: no match, confirmed]. Shape matrix over the JSON: the file still parses, load_registry and load_cloud_mappings both still load it, and the recorder's own 48 tests pass.

sweep_defs_read.note: sweeps: unrunnable (phase-2 source unreadable at <skill-dir>/../implement/phases/phase-2-implement.md). This branch predates the #529 bundle split and the executing engine here is the repo-own monolithic skills/review/SKILL.md; the installed devflow plugin's implement phase-2 sibling was not resolved from this worktree. Per the item-3b degraded arm this is recorded visibly rather than skipped silently, and Step 3.5 (blinded fix-delta gate) plus the Step 2.6 shadow are named as the covering backstop for this iteration's delta.

sweep_evidence.reason: phase-2 source unreadable from this worktree; backstops named (Step 3.5 + shadow)
performed_anyway: Class sweep (item 3): the VC-6 redaction finding was generalised from the one cited spelling to its whole defect_signature.kind class across the changed surface - env assignment, --flag, -u halves, and escaped quotes inside double quotes were all probed and fixed together, rather than fixing only the reported instance.

Coupled-site sweep: git grep + a whitespace-normalized (tr -s) sweep for 'eligible_lifecycles'/'eligible lifecycles' across .md/.py/.sh/.json found the docs enumeration in docs/workflow-flight-recorder.md, updated in the SAME commit; the changeset was updated in the same commit for the schema bump.

Mechanism-scoped comment re-sweep (item 3a): the fixes changed mechanisms (a regex, a filter predicate, a metric derivation), so the comments describing those mechanisms were re-verified and rewritten in place.

shadow.trigger: early (engine_self_modifying, after iteration 1; iter-1 routed to fixes so the convergence-time trigger had not run)

phase3_findings.description: SECRET_SHORT_U whole-operand-quoted alternatives not escape-aware -> -u "user:pa\"ss" leaks the tail
main() reports the fetch-level pagination_complete, contradicting the artifact it wrote and silencing its own warning
lifecycle_eligibility_state smuggled through the untyped provenance bag with a silent 'unrecorded' fallback
